page.title: List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province

revision.comment: 各国の式内社表の掲載。ただし、式内比定社は現在名神大社のみの状態、国史見在社・式外社も未掲載の状態。
revision.text:
'''安芸国の式内社一覧'''（あきのくにのしきないしゃいちらん）は、『[[延喜式]]』第9巻・第10巻「神名帳上下」（[[延喜式神名帳]]）に記載のある[[神社]]、いわゆる「[[式内社]]」およびその[[論社]]のうち、[[安芸国]]に分類されている神社の一覧。 また[[式外社]]として、当時すでに当国に存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社についても付記する。 == 式内社 == 『延喜式神名帳』には、大社3座3社（いずれも名神大社）が記載。 {{注|（凡例）
1）「神名帳」列は、[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年、近代デジタルライブラリー）等における『[[延喜式神名帳]]』の記載を基に作成。社名表記は[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjaindex.html 神社史料集成]における字体（異体字がある場合には新字体・通用性の高い字体を使用）を基準とした。読みの「-」部分は、「神社」以外で仮名が振られていない部分。「○座」は座数を表し、一座の場合は記載していない。
2）格の「名神大」は[[名神大社]]を、「大」は式内大社（名神大社除く）を、「小」は式内小社を意味する。付記は社名とともに記されているもので、その主要なものについては「[[式内社#式内社の社格]]」を参照。
3）比定社が複数ある場合、最も有力なものを無冠で示し、他の論社には「（論）」を冠した。
4）本来の式内社とは認めがたいものの、式内社を合祀したなど、その後継を主張するもの、その他参考の神社には「（参）」を冠した。}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; white-space:nowrap" align="left" !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | 神名帳 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | 比定社 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | 集成 |- !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 読み !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 格 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 付記 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 所在地 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 備考 |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[佐伯郡]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||ハヤタニノ||名神大||月次新嘗||[[速谷神社]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||イツキシマノ||名神大||||[[厳島神社]]||広島県廿日市市||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[安芸郡]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||オホイヘノ||名神大||||[[多家神社]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |} {{-}} == 式外社 == 当時すでに存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社。 === 国史見在社 === [[六国史]]に記載のある式外社。 {{節stub}} === その他 === 国史見在社以外の式外社。 {{節stub}} == 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー == 外部リンク == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjastate49.html 安芸国 式内社3座]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{式内社一覧}} [[Category:安芸国の式内社|*]]

revision.comment: /* 式内社 */ 凡例追記。
revision.text:
'''安芸国の式内社一覧'''（あきのくにのしきないしゃいちらん）は、『[[延喜式]]』第9巻・第10巻「神名帳上下」（[[延喜式神名帳]]）に記載のある[[神社]]、いわゆる「[[式内社]]」およびその[[論社]]のうち、[[安芸国]]に分類されている神社の一覧。 また[[式外社]]として、当時すでに当国に存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社についても付記する。 == 式内社 == 『延喜式神名帳』には、大社3座3社（いずれも名神大社）が記載。 {{注|（凡例）
1）「神名帳」列は、[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年、近代デジタルライブラリー）等における『[[延喜式神名帳]]』の記載を基に作成。社名表記は[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjaindex.html 神社史料集成]（5を参照）における字体（異体字がある場合には新字体・通用性の高い字体を使用）を基準とした。読みの「-」部分は、「神社」以外で仮名が振られていない部分。「○座」は座数を表し、一座の場合は記載していない。
2）格の「名神大」は[[名神大社]]を、「大」は式内大社（名神大社除く）を、「小」は式内小社を意味する。付記は社名とともに記されているもので、その主要なものについては「[[式内社#式内社の社格]]」を参照。
3）比定社が複数ある場合、最も有力なものを無冠で示し、他の論社には「（論）」を冠した。
4）本来の式内社とは認めがたいものの、式内社を合祀したなど、その後継を主張するもの、その他参考の神社には「（参）」を冠した。
5）「集成」列は[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjaindex.html 神社史料集成]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」）における神社項へのリンク先を記載。}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; white-space:nowrap" align="left" !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | 神名帳 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | 比定社 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | 集成 |- !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 読み !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 格 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 付記 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 所在地 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 備考 |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[佐伯郡]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||ハヤタニノ||名神大||月次新嘗||[[速谷神社]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||イツキシマノ||名神大||||[[厳島神社]]||広島県廿日市市||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[安芸郡]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||オホイヘノ||名神大||||[[多家神社]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |} {{-}} == 式外社 == 当時すでに存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社。 === 国史見在社 === [[六国史]]に記載のある式外社。 {{節stub}} === その他 === 国史見在社以外の式外社。 {{節stub}} == 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー == 外部リンク == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjastate49.html 安芸国 式内社3座]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{式内社一覧}} [[Category:安芸国の式内社|*]]

3）比定社が複数ある場合、最も有力なものを無冠で示し、他の論社には「（論）」を冠した。

5）「集成」列は[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjaindex.html 神社史料集成]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」）における神社項へのリンク先を記載。}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; white-space:nowrap" align="left" !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | 神名帳 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | 比定社 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | 集成 |- !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 読み !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 格 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 付記 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 所在地 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 備考 |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[佐伯郡]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||ハヤタニノ||名神大||月次新嘗||[[速谷神社]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||イツキシマノ||名神大||||[[厳島神社]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[安芸郡]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||オホイヘノ||名神大||||[[多家神社]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |} {{-}} == 式外社 == 当時すでに存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社。 === 国史見在社 === [[六国史]]に記載のある式外社。 {{節stub}} === その他 === 国史見在社以外の式外社。 {{節stub}} == 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー == 外部リンク == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjastate49.html 安芸国 式内社3座]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{式内社一覧}} [[Category:安芸国の式内社|*]]

revision.comment: 一宮等追記。
revision.text:

3）比定社が複数ある場合、最も有力なものを無冠で示し、他の論社には「（論）」を冠した。
4）本来の式内社とは認めがたいものの、式内社を合祀したなど、その後継を主張するもの、その他参考の神社には「（参）」を冠した。
5）「集成」列は[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjaindex.html 神社史料集成]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」）における神社項へのリンク先を記載。}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; white-space:nowrap" align="left" !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | 神名帳 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | 比定社 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | 集成 |- !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 読み !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 格 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 付記 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 所在地 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 備考 |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[佐伯郡]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||ハヤタニノ||名神大||月次新嘗||[[速谷神社]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||イツキシマノ||名神大||||[[厳島神社]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[安芸郡]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||オホイヘノ||名神大||||[[多家神社]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |} {{-}} == 式外社 == 当時すでに存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社。 === 国史見在社 === [[六国史]]に記載のある式外社。 {{節stub}} === その他 === 国史見在社以外の式外社。 {{節stub}} == 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー == 外部リンク == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjastate49.html 安芸国 式内社3座]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{式内社一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あきのくにのしきないしやいちらん}} [[Category:安芸国の式内社|*]] [[Category:式内社の一覧]]

revision.comment: /* 式内社 */
revision.text:
'''安芸国の式内社一覧'''（あきのくにのしきないしゃいちらん）は、『[[延喜式]]』第9巻・第10巻「神名帳上下」（[[延喜式神名帳]]）に記載のある[[神社]]、いわゆる「[[式内社]]」およびその[[論社]]のうち、[[安芸国]]に分類されている神社の一覧。 また[[式外社]]として、当時すでに当国に存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社についても付記する。 == 式内社 == 『延喜式神名帳』には、大社3座3社（いずれも名神大社）が記載。 {{注|（凡例）
1）「神名帳」列は、[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年、近代デジタルライブラリー）等における『[[延喜式神名帳]]』の記載を基に作成。社名表記は[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjaindex.html 神社史料集成]（5を参照）における字体（異体字がある場合には新字体・通用性の高い字体を使用）を基準とした。読みの「-」部分は、「神社」以外で仮名が振られていない部分。「○座」は座数を表し、一座の場合は記載していない。
2）格の「名神大」は[[名神大社]]を、「大」は式内大社（名神大社除く）を、「小」は式内小社を意味する。付記は社名とともに記されているもので、その主要なものについては「[[式内社#式内社の社格]]」を参照。
3）比定社が複数ある場合、最も有力なものを無冠で示し、他の論社には「（論）」を冠した。
4）本来の式内社とは認めがたいものの、式内社を合祀したなど、その後継を主張するもの、その他参考の神社には「（参）」を冠した。
5）「集成」列は[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjaindex.html 神社史料集成]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」）における神社項へのリンク先を記載。}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; white-space:nowrap" align="left" !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | 神名帳 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | 比定社 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | 集成 |- !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 読み !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 格 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 付記 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 社名 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 所在地 !align="center" style="background:#a0f0a0" | 備考 |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[佐伯郡]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||ハヤタニノ||名神大||月次新嘗||[[速谷神社]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||イツキシマノ||名神大||||[[厳島神社]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||オホイヘノ||名神大||||[[多家神社]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |} {{-}} == 式外社 == 当時すでに存在したはずであるのに『延喜式神名帳』に記載の無い神社。 === 国史見在社 === [[六国史]]に記載のある式外社。 {{節stub}} === その他 === 国史見在社以外の式外社。 {{節stub}} == 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1442211 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1273537 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 近代デジタルライブラリー == 外部リンク == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjastate49.html 安芸国 式内社3座]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{式内社一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あきのくにのしきないしやいちらん}} [[Category:安芸国の式内社|*]] [[Category:式内社の一覧]]

revision.comment: 式内社一覧ページの体裁訂正：説明文見直し、凡例および表定義のテンプレート化、国会図書館リンクの修正。
revision.text:
'''安芸国の式内社一覧'''（あきのくにのしきないしゃいちらん）は、『[[延喜式]]』第9巻・第10巻「神名帳上下」（[[延喜式神名帳]]）に記載のある[[神社]]、いわゆる「[[式内社]]」およびその[[論社]]のうち、[[安芸国]]に分類されている神社の一覧。
また『延喜式』神名帳の編纂当時に存在したが同帳に記載の無い神社、いわゆる「[[式外社]]」についても付記する。
== 式内社 == 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社3座3社（いずれも名神大社）を記載。
{{式内社一覧/凡例}} {{式内社一覧/header}} |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[佐伯郡]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||ハヤタニノ||名神大||月次新嘗||[[速谷神社]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||イツキシマノ||名神大||||[[厳島神社]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||オホイヘノ||名神大||||[[多家神社]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |}
== 式外社 == {{節stub}} 『延喜式』神名帳の編纂当時に存在したが、同帳に記載の無い神社。
== 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[{{NDLDC|1442211/160}} 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション * [{{NDLDC|1273537/37}} 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション
== 外部リンク == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjastate49.html 安芸国 式内社3座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」
{{式内社一覧}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:あきのくにのしきないしやいちらん}} [[Category:安芸国の式内社|*]] [[Category:式内社の一覧]]

revision.comment: adding explanatory comment
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X" then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise I have not. {{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X" then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise I have not. Translation of the texts will come later as it is a lot less difficult than the tables. Exception if there is another comment explaining the exception below[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 17 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Ooh this is so cool, this is the article on the three great shrines of Aki Province[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]])}} '''安芸国の式内社一覧'''（あきのくにのしきないしゃいちらん）は、『[[Engishiki]]』第9巻・第10巻「神名帳上下」（[[延喜式神名帳]]）に記載のある[[神社]]、いわゆる「[[式内社]]」およびその[[論社]]のうち、[[安芸国]]に分類されている神社の一覧。

So things might be a bit unexpected.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:01, 18 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X" then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise I have not. Translation of the texts will come later as it is a lot less difficult than the tables. Exception if there is another comment explaining the exception below[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 17 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Ooh this is so cool, this is the article on the three great shrines of Aki Province[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]])}} '''安芸国の式内社一覧'''（あきのくにのしきないしゃいちらん）は、『[[Engishiki]]』第9巻・第10巻「神名帳上下」（[[延喜式神名帳]]）に記載のある[[神社]]、いわゆる「[[式内社]]」およびその[[論社]]のうち、[[安芸国]]に分類されている神社の一覧。
また『延喜式』神名帳の編纂当時に存在したが同帳に記載の無い神社、いわゆる「[[式外社]]」についても付記する。
== 式内社 == 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社3座3社（いずれもMyojin Taisha社）を記載。

また『延喜式』神名帳の編纂当時に存在したが同帳に記載の無い神社、いわゆる「[[式外社]]」についても付記する。

{{式内社一覧/凡例}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[:en:Saeki_District,_Hiroshima|Saeki]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||hayatanino||Myojin Taisha||月次新嘗||[[Hayatani Shrine]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||itsukishimano||Myojin Taisha||||[[Itsukushima Shrine]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[Aki District, Hiroshima|Aki]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||ohoiheno||Myojin Taisha||||[[Take Shrine]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |}
== Content from Take Shrine == '''Take Shrine''' (多家神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in [[Fuchū, Hiroshima (town)|Fuchu]], [[Aki District, Hiroshima]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
It is a [[Sōja shrine]] so it enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Aki Province|Aki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located on the site of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" />
It was mentioned in 927 in the [[Engishiki]] and highly ranked as a [[Myojin Taisha]]..<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref>
There used to be an earlier [[Soja Shrine]] before it was moved here
This shrine is one of the "Three Great Shrines of Aki Province", along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Hayatani Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref>
According to shrine tradition, the original Take Shrine was established on the site of the [[Takemikazuchi]] Palace (mentioned in the Kojiki, an ancient Japanese text) or Enomiya (mentioned in the Nihon Shoki, another ancient text), where Emperor Jinmu stayed for seven years during his eastern expedition.<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":0" /> However the status of the shrine gradually declined over time from being one of the three great shrines, and its location was lost during the [[Edo period]].<ref name=":0" />
Its treasure house is one of the few remaining buildings of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" /> The Take Shrine's treasure house managed to survive a fire in 1915.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Take Shrine's Treasure House in the Azekura Style -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/021.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><!--Content taken from [[Sōja (Shinto)]] explaining the concept of it-->
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-11-21|title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|access-date=2023-11-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|archive-date=2023-11-21}}</ref>
{{nihongo|Sōja|総社|}} is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] where the ''[[kami]]'' of a region are grouped together into a single sanctuary. This "region" may refer to a ''[[shōen]]'', village or geographic area, but is more generally referred to a whole [[Provinces of Japan|province]]. The term is also occasionally called "sōsha". The ''sōja'' are usually located near the [[Kokufu|provincial capital]] established in the [[Nara period]] under then ''[[ritsuryō]]'' system, and can either be a newly created shrine, or a designation for an existing shrine. The "sōja" can also be the "[[ichinomiya]]" of the province, which themselves are of great ritual importance.<ref name="Herbert">{{cite book|last1=Herbert|first1=Jean|title=Shinto:At the Fountain-head of Japan|date=2011|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-0-415-59348-9|page=105}}</ref>
Whenever a new [[Kokushi (official)|kokushi]] was appointed by the central government to govern a province, it was necessary for him to visit all of the sanctuaries of his province in order to complete the rites necessary for ceremonial inauguration. Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>
The first mention of "sōja" appeared in the [[Heian period]], in the diary of Taira no Tokinori, dated March 9, 1099 in reference to the province of [[Inaba]].<ref name="Hardacre">{{cite book|last1=Hardacre|first1=Helen|title=Shinto: A History|date=2016|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0190621711}}</ref>
== Content from Hayatani Shrine == ''''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].
=== Overview === Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> It used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" /> The shrine is also known for purifying the buses and trains of the [[Hiroshima Electric Railway]].
This shrine is one of the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]", along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> For a long time, travelers on the [[San'yōdō|Sanyo Expressway]] have prayed for safety on their journeys at this shrine, and it is still considered a protector of traffic safety on the modern day [[San'yō Expressway|expressway]].
It and Itsukushima shrine used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref>
== 式外社 == {{節stub}} 『延喜式』神名帳の編纂当時に存在したが、同帳に記載の無い神社。

This article is a list of shrines in Aki Province that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, "Shinmeichō Jōge", of the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}). It comprises the shrines known as the so-called {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and their associated {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, which are classified under {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}.
It also includes those shrines, referred to as {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社}}, that existed at the time of the compilation of the Engishiki Divine Name Register but were not recorded in the register.
[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]
== 式内社 == 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社3座3社（いずれもMyojin Taisha社）を記載。

== Content from Take Shrine == '''Take Shrine''' (多家神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in [[Fuchū, Hiroshima (town)|Fuchu]], [[Aki District, Hiroshima]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
It is a [[Sōja shrine]] so it enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Aki Province|Aki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located on the site of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" />
It was mentioned in 927 in the [[Engishiki]] and highly ranked as a [[Myojin Taisha]]..<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref>
There used to be an earlier [[Soja Shrine]] before it was moved here
This shrine is one of the "Three Great Shrines of Aki Province", along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Hayatani Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref>
According to shrine tradition, the original Take Shrine was established on the site of the [[Takemikazuchi]] Palace (mentioned in the Kojiki, an ancient Japanese text) or Enomiya (mentioned in the Nihon Shoki, another ancient text), where Emperor Jinmu stayed for seven years during his eastern expedition.<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":0" /> However the status of the shrine gradually declined over time from being one of the three great shrines, and its location was lost during the [[Edo period]].<ref name=":0" />
Its treasure house is one of the few remaining buildings of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" /> The Take Shrine's treasure house managed to survive a fire in 1915.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Take Shrine's Treasure House in the Azekura Style -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/021.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><!--Content taken from [[Sōja (Shinto)]] explaining the concept of it-->
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-11-21|title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|access-date=2023-11-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|archive-date=2023-11-21}}</ref>
{{nihongo|Sōja|総社|}} is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] where the ''[[kami]]'' of a region are grouped together into a single sanctuary. This "region" may refer to a ''[[shōen]]'', village or geographic area, but is more generally referred to a whole [[Provinces of Japan|province]]. The term is also occasionally called "sōsha". The ''sōja'' are usually located near the [[Kokufu|provincial capital]] established in the [[Nara period]] under then ''[[ritsuryō]]'' system, and can either be a newly created shrine, or a designation for an existing shrine. The "sōja" can also be the "[[ichinomiya]]" of the province, which themselves are of great ritual importance.<ref name="Herbert">{{cite book|last1=Herbert|first1=Jean|title=Shinto:At the Fountain-head of Japan|date=2011|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-0-415-59348-9|page=105}}</ref>
Whenever a new [[Kokushi (official)|kokushi]] was appointed by the central government to govern a province, it was necessary for him to visit all of the sanctuaries of his province in order to complete the rites necessary for ceremonial inauguration. Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>
The first mention of "sōja" appeared in the [[Heian period]], in the diary of Taira no Tokinori, dated March 9, 1099 in reference to the province of [[Inaba]].<ref name="Hardacre">{{cite book|last1=Hardacre|first1=Helen|title=Shinto: A History|date=2016|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0190621711}}</ref>
== Content from Hayatani Shrine == ''''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].

[[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]] It also includes those shrines, referred to as {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社}}, that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} but were not recorded in the register.
== 式内社 == 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社3座3社（いずれもMyojin Taisha社）を記載。
{{式内社一覧/凡例}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[:en:Saeki_District,_Hiroshima|Saeki]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||hayatanino||Myojin Taisha||月次新嘗||[[Hayatani Shrine]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||itsukishimano||Myojin Taisha||||[[Itsukushima Shrine]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[Aki District, Hiroshima|Aki]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||ohoiheno||Myojin Taisha||||[[Take Shrine]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |}
== Content from Take Shrine == '''Take Shrine''' (多家神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in [[Fuchū, Hiroshima (town)|Fuchu]], [[Aki District, Hiroshima]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
It is a [[Sōja shrine]] so it enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Aki Province|Aki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located on the site of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" />
It was mentioned in 927 in the [[Engishiki]] and highly ranked as a [[Myojin Taisha]]..<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref>
There used to be an earlier [[Soja Shrine]] before it was moved here
This shrine is one of the "Three Great Shrines of Aki Province", along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Hayatani Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref>
According to shrine tradition, the original Take Shrine was established on the site of the [[Takemikazuchi]] Palace (mentioned in the Kojiki, an ancient Japanese text) or Enomiya (mentioned in the Nihon Shoki, another ancient text), where Emperor Jinmu stayed for seven years during his eastern expedition.<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":0" /> However the status of the shrine gradually declined over time from being one of the three great shrines, and its location was lost during the [[Edo period]].<ref name=":0" />
Its treasure house is one of the few remaining buildings of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" /> The Take Shrine's treasure house managed to survive a fire in 1915.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Take Shrine's Treasure House in the Azekura Style -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/021.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><!--Content taken from [[Sōja (Shinto)]] explaining the concept of it-->
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-11-21|title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|access-date=2023-11-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|archive-date=2023-11-21}}</ref>
{{nihongo|Sōja|総社|}} is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] where the ''[[kami]]'' of a region are grouped together into a single sanctuary. This "region" may refer to a ''[[shōen]]'', village or geographic area, but is more generally referred to a whole [[Provinces of Japan|province]]. The term is also occasionally called "sōsha". The ''sōja'' are usually located near the [[Kokufu|provincial capital]] established in the [[Nara period]] under then ''[[ritsuryō]]'' system, and can either be a newly created shrine, or a designation for an existing shrine. The "sōja" can also be the "[[ichinomiya]]" of the province, which themselves are of great ritual importance.<ref name="Herbert">{{cite book|last1=Herbert|first1=Jean|title=Shinto:At the Fountain-head of Japan|date=2011|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-0-415-59348-9|page=105}}</ref>
Whenever a new [[Kokushi (official)|kokushi]] was appointed by the central government to govern a province, it was necessary for him to visit all of the sanctuaries of his province in order to complete the rites necessary for ceremonial inauguration. Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>
The first mention of "sōja" appeared in the [[Heian period]], in the diary of Taira no Tokinori, dated March 9, 1099 in reference to the province of [[Inaba]].<ref name="Hardacre">{{cite book|last1=Hardacre|first1=Helen|title=Shinto: A History|date=2016|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0190621711}}</ref>
== Content from Hayatani Shrine == ''''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].
=== Overview === Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> It used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" /> The shrine is also known for purifying the buses and trains of the [[Hiroshima Electric Railway]].
This shrine is one of the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]", along with [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> For a long time, travelers on the [[San'yōdō|Sanyo Expressway]] have prayed for safety on their journeys at this shrine, and it is still considered a protector of traffic safety on the modern day [[San'yō Expressway|expressway]].
It and Itsukushima shrine used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref>
== 式外社 == {{節stub}} 『延喜式』神名帳の編纂当時に存在したが、同帳に記載の無い神社。
== 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[{{NDLDC|1442211/160}} 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 国立国会図書館デジtarukorekushiョn * [{{NDLDC|1273537/37}} 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 国立国会図書館デジtarukorekushiョn
== 外部rinku == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/jinjastate49.html 安芸国 式内社3座] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプroグramu「神道・神社史料集成」
{{式内社一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}{{afc comment|1=Switching over to deprecating the templates. So things might be a bit unexpected.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:01, 18 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X" then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise I have not. Translation of the texts will come later as it is a lot less difficult than the tables. Exception if there is another comment explaining the exception below[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 17 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Ooh this is so cool, this is the article on the three great shrines of Aki Province[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]])}}
This article is a list of shrines in Aki Province that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, "Shinmeichō Jōge", of the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}). It comprises the shrines known as the so-called {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and their associated {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, which are classified under {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}.
It also includes those shrines, referred to as {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社}}, that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} but were not recorded in the register.
There are only three Shikinaisha in Aki Province. These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> [[category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]] == 式内社 == 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社3座3社（いずれもMyojin Taisha社）を記載。
{{式内社一覧/凡例}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[:en:Saeki_District,_Hiroshima|Saeki]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||hayatanino||Myojin Taisha||月次新嘗||[[Hayatani Shrine]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||itsukishimano||Myojin Taisha||||[[Itsukushima Shrine]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[Aki District, Hiroshima|Aki]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||ohoiheno||Myojin Taisha||||[[Take Shrine]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |}

Its treasure house is one of the few remaining buildings of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" /> The Take Shrine's treasure house managed to survive a fire in 1915.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Take Shrine's Treasure House in the Azekura Style -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/021.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><!--Content taken from [[Sōja (Shinto)]] explaining the concept of it-->

{{nihongo|Sōja|総社|}} is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] where the ''[[kami]]'' of a region are grouped together into a single sanctuary. This "region" may refer to a ''[[shōen]]'', village or geographic area, but is more generally referred to a whole [[Provinces of Japan|province]]. The term is also occasionally called "sōsha". The ''sōja'' are usually located near the [[Kokufu|provincial capital]] established in the [[Nara period]] under then ''[[ritsuryō]]'' system, and can either be a newly created shrine, or a designation for an existing shrine. The "sōja" can also be the "[[ichinomiya]]" of the province, which themselves are of great ritual importance.<ref name="Herbert">{{cite book|last1=Herbert|first1=Jean|title=Shinto:At the Fountain-head of Japan|date=2011|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-0-415-59348-9|page=105}}</ref>
Whenever a new [[Kokushi (official)|kokushi]] was appointed by the central government to govern a province, it was necessary for him to visit all of the sanctuaries of his province in order to complete the rites necessary for ceremonial inauguration. Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>
The first mention of "sōja" appeared in the [[Heian period]], in the diary of Taira no Tokinori, dated March 9, 1099 in reference to the province of [[Inaba]].<ref name="Hardacre">{{cite book|last1=Hardacre|first1=Helen|title=Shinto: A History|date=2016|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0190621711}}</ref>
== Content from Hayatani Shrine == '''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].

It and Itsukushima shrine used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref>

revision.comment: Moved stuff up
revision.text:
{{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}{{afc comment|1=Switching over to deprecating the templates. So things might be a bit unexpected.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:01, 18 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X" then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise I have not. Translation of the texts will come later as it is a lot less difficult than the tables. Exception if there is another comment explaining the exception below[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 17 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Ooh this is so cool, this is the article on the three great shrines of Aki Province[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]])}}
This article is a list of shrines in Aki Province that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, "Shinmeichō Jōge", of the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}). It comprises the shrines known as the so-called {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and their associated {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, which are classified under {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}.
It also includes those shrines, referred to as {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社}}, that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} but were not recorded in the register.
There are only three Shikinaisha in Aki Province. These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref>
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]] == 式内社 == 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社3座3社（いずれもMyojin Taisha社）を記載。
{{式内社一覧/凡例}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[:en:Saeki_District,_Hiroshima|Saeki]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||hayatanino||Myojin Taisha||月次新嘗||[[Hayatani Shrine]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||itsukishimano||Myojin Taisha||||[[Itsukushima Shrine]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[Aki District, Hiroshima|Aki]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||ohoiheno||Myojin Taisha||||[[Take Shrine]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |}
== Content from Take Shrine == '''Take Shrine''' (多家神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in [[Fuchū, Hiroshima (town)|Fuchu]], [[Aki District, Hiroshima]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
It is a [[Sōja shrine]] so it enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Aki Province|Aki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located on the site of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" />
It was mentioned in 927 in the [[Engishiki]] and highly ranked as a [[Myojin Taisha]]..<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref>
There used to be an earlier [[Soja Shrine]] before it was moved here
According to shrine tradition, the original Take Shrine was established on the site of the [[Takemikazuchi]] Palace (mentioned in the Kojiki, an ancient Japanese text) or Enomiya (mentioned in the Nihon Shoki, another ancient text), where Emperor Jinmu stayed for seven years during his eastern expedition.<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":0" /> However the status of the shrine gradually declined over time from being one of the three great shrines, and its location was lost during the [[Edo period]].<ref name=":0" />
Its treasure house is one of the few remaining buildings of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" /> The Take Shrine's treasure house managed to survive a fire in 1915.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Take Shrine's Treasure House in the Azekura Style -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/021.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><!--Content taken from [[Sōja (Shinto)]] explaining the concept of it-->
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-11-21|title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|access-date=2023-11-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|archive-date=2023-11-21}}</ref>
{{nihongo|Sōja|総社|}} is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] where the ''[[kami]]'' of a region are grouped together into a single sanctuary. This "region" may refer to a ''[[shōen]]'', village or geographic area, but is more generally referred to a whole [[Provinces of Japan|province]]. The term is also occasionally called "sōsha". The ''sōja'' are usually located near the [[Kokufu|provincial capital]] established in the [[Nara period]] under then ''[[ritsuryō]]'' system, and can either be a newly created shrine, or a designation for an existing shrine. The "sōja" can also be the "[[ichinomiya]]" of the province, which themselves are of great ritual importance.<ref name="Herbert">{{cite book|last1=Herbert|first1=Jean|title=Shinto:At the Fountain-head of Japan|date=2011|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-0-415-59348-9|page=105}}</ref>
Whenever a new [[Kokushi (official)|kokushi]] was appointed by the central government to govern a province, it was necessary for him to visit all of the sanctuaries of his province in order to complete the rites necessary for ceremonial inauguration. Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>
The first mention of "sōja" appeared in the [[Heian period]], in the diary of Taira no Tokinori, dated March 9, 1099 in reference to the province of [[Inaba]].<ref name="Hardacre">{{cite book|last1=Hardacre|first1=Helen|title=Shinto: A History|date=2016|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0190621711}}</ref>
== Content from Hayatani Shrine == '''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].
=== Overview === Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" /> The shrine is also known for purifying the buses and trains of the [[Hiroshima Electric Railway]].
This shrine is one of the
For a long time, travelers on the [[San'yōdō|Sanyo Expressway]] have prayed for safety on their journeys at this shrine, and it is still considered a protector of traffic safety on the modern day [[San'yō Expressway|expressway]].
== 参考文献 == * 皇典講究所・全国神職会校訂[{{NDLDC|1442211/160}} 『延喜式 上巻』]（大岡山書店、昭和4年） - 国立国会図書館デジtarukorekushiョn * [{{NDLDC|1273537/37}} 『延喜式 第2』]（日本古典全集刊行会、昭和4年） - 国立国会図書館デジtarukorekushiョn

Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>

== Content from Hayatani Shrine == '''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].
=== Overview === Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" /> The shrine is also known for purifying the buses and trains of the [[Hiroshima Electric Railway]].
This shrine is one of the
For a long time, travelers on the [[San'yōdō|Sanyo Expressway]] have prayed for safety on their journeys at this shrine, and it is still considered a protector of traffic safety on the modern day [[San'yō Expressway|expressway]].

{{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}{{afc comment|1=Switching over to deprecating the templates. So things might be a bit unexpected.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:01, 18 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=If this is in the format "List of Shikinaisha in X" then I have linked all existing shrines into the table. Otherwise I have not. Translation of the texts will come later as it is a lot less difficult than the tables. Exception if there is another comment explaining the exception below[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 17 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Ooh this is so cool, this is the article on the three great shrines of Aki Province[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]])}}
This article is a list of shrines in Aki Province that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, "Shinmeichō Jōge", of the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}). It comprises the shrines known as the so-called {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and their associated {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社}}, which are classified under {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}.
It also includes those shrines, referred to as {{ill|Shikigeisha|ja|式外社}}, that existed at the time of the compilation of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} but were not recorded in the register.
There are only three Shikinaisha in Aki Province. These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref>
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]] == 式内社 == 『延喜式』神名帳では、大社3座3社（いずれもMyojin Taisha社）を記載。
{{List of Shikinai Shrines/Legend}} {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff; font-size:85%" ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=4 | {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} ! style="background:#a0f0a0" colspan=3 | Designated Shrines ! style="background:#a0f0a0" rowspan=2 | Compilation |- style="white-space:nowrap;" ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Reading ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Rank ! style="background:#a0f0a0;" | Note ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.6em; padding-left:2.6em;" | Shrine Name ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.1em; padding-left:2.1em;" | Location ! style="background:#a0f0a0; padding-right:2.3em; padding-left:2.3em;" | Remarks |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[:en:Saeki_District,_Hiroshima|Saeki]]''' 2座（並大） |- |'''速谷神社'''||hayatanino||Myojin Taisha||月次新嘗||[[Hayatani Shrine]]||[[広島県]][[廿日市市]]上平良||（安芸国二宮）||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490201.html] |- |'''伊都伎島神社'''||itsukishimano||Myojin Taisha||||[[Itsukushima Shrine]]||広島県廿日市市宮島町||安芸国[[一宮]]||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490101.html] |- |colspan=8 style="background-color:#ccffcc;"|'''[[Aki District, Hiroshima|Aki]]''' 1座（大） |- |'''多家神社'''||ohoiheno||Myojin Taisha||||[[Take Shrine]]||広島県[[安芸郡 (広島県)|安芸郡]][[府中町]]宮の町||安芸国[[総社]]後継社||[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/490301.html] |}
== Content from Take Shrine == '''Take Shrine''' (多家神社) is a [[Sōja shrine]] in [[Fuchū, Hiroshima (town)|Fuchu]], [[Aki District, Hiroshima]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
It is a [[Sōja shrine]] so it enshrines all the [[kami]] of the shrines in [[Aki Province|Aki]] [[Provinces of Japan|Province]]. It is located on the site of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" />
It was mentioned in 927 in the [[Engishiki]] and highly ranked as a [[Myojin Taisha]]..<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref>
There used to be an earlier [[Soja Shrine]] before it was moved here
According to shrine tradition, the original Take Shrine was established on the site of the [[Takemikazuchi]] Palace (mentioned in the Kojiki, an ancient Japanese text) or Enomiya (mentioned in the Nihon Shoki, another ancient text), where Emperor Jinmu stayed for seven years during his eastern expedition.<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":0" /> However the status of the shrine gradually declined over time from being one of the three great shrines, and its location was lost during the [[Edo period]].<ref name=":0" />
Its treasure house is one of the few remaining buildings of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" /> The Take Shrine's treasure house managed to survive a fire in 1915.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Take Shrine's Treasure House in the Azekura Style -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/021.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><!--Content taken from [[Sōja (Shinto)]] explaining the concept of it-->
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-11-21|title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|access-date=2023-11-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|archive-date=2023-11-21}}</ref>
{{nihongo|Sōja|総社|}} is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] where the ''[[kami]]'' of a region are grouped together into a single sanctuary. This "region" may refer to a ''[[shōen]]'', village or geographic area, but is more generally referred to a whole [[Provinces of Japan|province]]. The term is also occasionally called "sōsha". The ''sōja'' are usually located near the [[Kokufu|provincial capital]] established in the [[Nara period]] under then ''[[ritsuryō]]'' system, and can either be a newly created shrine, or a designation for an existing shrine. The "sōja" can also be the "[[ichinomiya]]" of the province, which themselves are of great ritual importance.<ref name="Herbert">{{cite book|last1=Herbert|first1=Jean|title=Shinto:At the Fountain-head of Japan|date=2011|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-0-415-59348-9|page=105}}</ref>
Whenever a new [[Kokushi (official)|kokushi]] was appointed by the central government to govern a province, it was necessary for him to visit all of the sanctuaries of his province in order to complete the rites necessary for ceremonial inauguration. Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>
The first mention of "sōja" appeared in the [[Heian period]], in the diary of Taira no Tokinori, dated March 9, 1099 in reference to the province of [[Inaba]].<ref name="Hardacre">{{cite book|last1=Hardacre|first1=Helen|title=Shinto: A History|date=2016|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0190621711}}</ref>
== Content from Hayatani Shrine == '''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].
=== Overview === Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" /> The shrine is also known for purifying the buses and trains of the [[Hiroshima Electric Railway]].
This shrine is one of the
For a long time, travelers on the [[San'yōdō|Sanyo Expressway]] have prayed for safety on their journeys at this shrine, and it is still considered a protector of traffic safety on the modern day [[San'yō Expressway|expressway]].

It was mentioned in 927 in the [[Engishiki]] and highly ranked as a [[Myojin Taisha]]..<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref>
There used to be an earlier [[Soja Shrine]] before it was moved here
According to shrine tradition, the original Take Shrine was established on the site of the [[Takemikazuchi]] Palace (mentioned in the Kojiki, an ancient Japanese text) or Enomiya (mentioned in the Nihon Shoki, another ancient text), where Emperor Jinmu stayed for seven years during his eastern expedition.<ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":0" /> However the status of the shrine gradually declined over time from being one of the three great shrines, and its location was lost during the [[Edo period]].<ref name=":0" />
Its treasure house is one of the few remaining buildings of [[Hiroshima Castle]].<ref name=":0" /> The Take Shrine's treasure house managed to survive a fire in 1915.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Take Shrine's Treasure House in the Azekura Style -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/021.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><!--Content taken from [[Sōja (Shinto)]] explaining the concept of it-->
Ichinomiya and Soja are not the same thing but were sometimes combined<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-11-21|title=Ichinomiya / Sōja {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|access-date=2023-11-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121195632/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8841|archive-date=2023-11-21}}</ref>
{{nihongo|Sōja|総社|}} is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] where the ''[[kami]]'' of a region are grouped together into a single sanctuary. This "region" may refer to a ''[[shōen]]'', village or geographic area, but is more generally referred to a whole [[Provinces of Japan|province]]. The term is also occasionally called "sōsha". The ''sōja'' are usually located near the [[Kokufu|provincial capital]] established in the [[Nara period]] under then ''[[ritsuryō]]'' system, and can either be a newly created shrine, or a designation for an existing shrine. The "sōja" can also be the "[[ichinomiya]]" of the province, which themselves are of great ritual importance.<ref name="Herbert">{{cite book|last1=Herbert|first1=Jean|title=Shinto:At the Fountain-head of Japan|date=2011|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-0-415-59348-9|page=105}}</ref>
Whenever a new [[Kokushi (official)|kokushi]] was appointed by the central government to govern a province, it was necessary for him to visit all of the sanctuaries of his province in order to complete the rites necessary for ceremonial inauguration. Grouping the ''kami'' into one location near the capital of the province greatly facilitated this duty,<ref name="Bocking">{{cite book|last1=Bocking|first1=Brian|title=A Popular Dictionary of Shinto|date=2016|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1138979079}}</ref>
The first mention of "sōja" appeared in the [[Heian period]], in the diary of Taira no Tokinori, dated March 9, 1099 in reference to the province of [[Inaba]].<ref name="Hardacre">{{cite book|last1=Hardacre|first1=Helen|title=Shinto: A History|date=2016|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0190621711}}</ref>
== Content from Hayatani Shrine == '''Hayatani Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Hatsukaichi, a suburb of [[Hiroshima]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-03-03|title=Hayatani Jinja|url=http://yuugenguide.squarespace.com/shrines/2018/12/20/hayatani-jinja|access-date=2023-12-02|website=Yuugen 幽玄|language=en-US}}</ref> It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QmdRAAAAYAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Engi-shiki; Procedures of the Engi Era: Books VI-X|date=1972|publisher=Sophia University|language=en}}</ref> and [[Ichinomiya|Ninomiya]] of [[Aki Province]]<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref> and is currently designated as a [[Beppyo Shrine]].
=== Overview === Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" /> The shrine is also known for purifying the buses and trains of the [[Hiroshima Electric Railway]].
This shrine is one of the
For a long time, travelers on the [[San'yōdō|Sanyo Expressway]] have prayed for safety on their journeys at this shrine, and it is still considered a protector of traffic safety on the modern day [[San'yō Expressway|expressway]].
