{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451199]]
[[:ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:03, 27 July 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}
This article is a list of shrines in [[Aki Province]] that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳|simple|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}).
There are three shrines in this list. All three of these shrines are Myojin Taisha.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref name=":4" />
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[Itsukushima Shrine]] is the [[Ichinomiya]],<ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''", p. 3] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf|date=2013-05-17}}; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref> and [[Take Shrine]] is the [[Sōja shrine]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[:en:Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the Rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" />
The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 3 Shikinaisha in the province – 0 Shikinai Shosha and 3 Shikinai Taisha.
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! Same as !! Co-ords |- | 2575 | 1 | Saeki district | {{ill|Hayatani Shrine|WD=Q11639721}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183120 archive] | Tsukinami-/Niiname-sai | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.359164|132.308749|display=inline}} |- | 2576 | 2 | Saeki district | {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183121 archive] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.295770|132.320105|display=inline}} |- | 2577 | 3 | Aki district | {{ill|Take Shrine|WD=Q11430665}} | たけじんじゃ | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183122 archive] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.396035|132.510499|display=inline}} |}
== Shikigeisha (式外社) == ''(none listed)''

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451199]]
[[:ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:03, 27 July 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}
This article is a list of shrines in [[Aki Province]] that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳|simple|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}).
There are three shrines in this list. All three of these shrines are Myōjin Taisha.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref name=":4" />
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[Itsukushima Shrine]] is the [[Ichinomiya]],<ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''", p. 3] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf|date=2013-05-17}}; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref> and [[Take Shrine]] is the [[Sōja shrine]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the Rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" />
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! Same as !! Co-ords |- | 2575 | 1 | Saeki district | {{ill|Hayatani Shrine|WD=Q11639721}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183120 archive] | Tsukinami-/Niiname-sai | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.359164|132.308749|display=inline}} |- | 2576 | 2 | Saeki district | {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183121 archive] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.295770|132.320105|display=inline}} |- | 2577 | 3 | Aki district | {{ill|Take Shrine|WD=Q11430665}} | たけじんじゃ | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183122 archive] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.396035|132.510499|display=inline}} |}
== References == <references />{{translated page|ja|安芸国の式内社一覧|version=56551135|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{List of Shikinaisha}} [[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]] [[d:Q11451199]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Aki Province]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - " archive]↵" to " DB] "
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451199]]
[[:ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:03, 27 July 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}
This article is a list of shrines in [[Aki Province]] that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳|simple|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}).
There are three shrines in this list. All three of these shrines are Myōjin Taisha.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref name=":4" />
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[Itsukushima Shrine]] is the [[Ichinomiya]],<ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''", p. 3] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf|date=2013-05-17}}; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref> and [[Take Shrine]] is the [[Sōja shrine]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the Rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" />
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! Same as !! Coordinates |- | 2575 | 1 | Saeki district | {{ill|Hayatani Shrine|WD=Q11639721}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183120 DB] | Tsukinami-/Niiname-sai | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.359164|132.308749|display=inline}} |- | 2576 | 2 | Saeki district | {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183121 DB] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.295770|132.320105|display=inline}} |- | 2577 | 3 | Aki district | {{ill|Take Shrine|WD=Q11430665}} | たけじんじゃ | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183122 DB] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.396035|132.510499|display=inline}} |}
== References == <references />{{translated page|ja|安芸国の式内社一覧|version=56551135|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{List of Shikinaisha}} [[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]] [[d:Q11451199]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Aki Province]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:03, 27 July 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}
This article is a list of shrines in [[Aki Province]] that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳|simple|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}).
There are three shrines in this list. All three of these shrines are Myōjin Taisha.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref name=":4" />
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[Itsukushima Shrine]] is the [[Ichinomiya]],<ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''", p. 3] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf|date=2013-05-17}}; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref> and [[Take Shrine]] is the [[Sōja shrine]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the Rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" />

Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[Itsukushima Shrine]] is the [[Ichinomiya]],<ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''", p. 3] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf|date=2013-05-17}}; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref> and [[Take Shrine]] is the [[Sōja shrine]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the Rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" />

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451199]]
[[:ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:03, 27 July 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}
This article is a list of shrines in [[Aki Province]] that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳|simple|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}).
There are three shrines in this list. All three of these shrines are Myōjin Taisha.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref name=":4" />
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[Itsukushima Shrine]] is the [[Ichinomiya]],<ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''", p. 3] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf|date=2013-05-17}}; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref> and [[Take Shrine]] is the [[Sōja shrine]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the Rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" />
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! Same as !! Coordinates |- | 2575 | 1 | Saeki district | {{ill|Hayatani Shrine|WD=Q11639721}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183120 archive] | Tsukinami-/Niiname-sai | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.359164|132.308749|display=inline}} |- | 2576 | 2 | Saeki district | {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183121 archive] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.295770|132.320105|display=inline}} |- | 2577 | 3 | Aki district | {{ill|Take Shrine|WD=Q11430665}} | たけじんじゃ | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183122 archive] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.396035|132.510499|display=inline}} |}
== References == <references />{{translated page|ja|安芸国の式内社一覧|version=56551135|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{List of Shikinaisha}} [[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]] [[d:Q11451199]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Aki Province]] [[Category:Lists of Shikinaisha by location]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451199]]
[[:ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:03, 27 July 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Three Great Shrines of Aki Province}}
This article is a list of shrines in [[Aki Province]] that are recorded in Volume 9 and Volume 10, ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|zh|延喜式神名帳|simple|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}).
There are three shrines in this list. All three of these shrines are Myōjin Taisha.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|url=https://aki-fuchu.jp/english/introduction_e_right.htm|access-date=2023-12-02|website=aki-fuchu.jp}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPq-lhGqzgEC&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=Hiroshima|date=1986|publisher=Hiroshima City|language=en}}</ref> These are called the "Three Great Shrines of [[Aki Province]]" and include [[Hayatani Shrine]], [[Itsukushima Shrine]] and [[Take Shrine]].<ref name=":4" />
Take Shrine used to have a higher status than [[Itsukushima Shrine]],<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a">{{Cite web|title=Hiroshima Cultural Encyclopedia - Gokurakuji Mountain -|url=http://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/020.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.hiroshima-bunka.jp}}</ref><ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a">{{Cite web|url=https://mydaytrip.com/discover/location/hayatani-shrine|access-date=2023-12-02|website=mydaytrip.com|title=Discover Hayatani Shrine}}</ref> but due to the support of the [[Taira clan]] it was eclipsed.<ref name="wwwhiroshimabunkajp-2023a" /> The two shrines used to simultaneously provide offerings in a single ceremony.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Havens|first1=Norman|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a_YQAQAAIAAJ&q=Hayatani+Shrine|title=An Encyclopedia of Shinto (Shinto Jiten): Kami|last2=Inoue|first2=Nobutaka|date=2006|publisher=Institute for Japanese Culture and Classics Kokugakuin University|isbn=978-4-905853-08-4|language=en}}</ref> [[Itsukushima Shrine]] is the [[Ichinomiya]],<ref>[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf "Nationwide List of ''Ichinomiya''", p. 3] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517061440/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Ab.pdf|date=2013-05-17}}; retrieved 2012-11-20.</ref> and [[Take Shrine]] is the [[Sōja shrine]]. <ref name=":1">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=shFDAQAAIAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=The Japan Daily Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VB9CAQAAMAAJ&q=%22take+shrine%22+hiroshima|title=Japan Weekly Mail|date=1899|language=en}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Take Shrine Treasure House - Hiroshimatatemonogatari|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/tatemonogatari/en/page062.html|access-date=2023-12-02|website=www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp}}</ref>
Hayatani Shrine is a sacred place in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]] that has been worshipped for over 1,700 years. It was listed as a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] before the Engishiki was written, In 811 it and [[Itsukushima]] were both given the Rank by the Emperor.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2023-03-10|title=Myōjin {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|access-date=2023-12-02|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230310155140/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9984|archive-date=2023-03-10}}</ref> People from all over Japan visit Hayatani Shrine to pray for [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]], especially when buying a new car.<ref name="mydaytripcom-2023a" />
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! Global !! Prov !! District !! Name !! Kana !! Rank !! Shrine DB !! Celebration !! Seats !! Subtype !! Parent shrine !! Same as !! Coordinates |- | 2575 | 1 | Saeki district | {{ill|Hayatani Shrine|WD=Q11639721}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183120 DB] | Tsukinami-/Niiname-sai | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.359164|132.308749|display=inline}} |- | 2576 | 2 | Saeki district | {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763}} | | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183121 DB] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.295770|132.320105|display=inline}} |- | 2577 | 3 | Aki district | {{ill|Take Shrine|WD=Q11430665}} | たけじんじゃ | Myōjin Taisha | [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183122 DB] | — | single | — | — | — | {{coord|34.396035|132.510499|display=inline}} |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]][[simple:List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451199]]

[[:ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 21 November 2025 (UTC)}}
This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Aki Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 3 Shikinaisha in the province – 0 Shikinai Shosha and 3 Shikinai Taisha.

== Shikinaisha (式内社) ==
{| class="wikitable sortable"
! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB

| {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|WD=Q135009132|lt=Tsukinami}}{{efn|gets offerings for the yearly {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|WD=Q11501518}} and the monthly {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|WD=Q11516161}}.}})

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 3 Shikinaisha in the province – 0 Shikinai Shosha and 3 Shikinai Taisha.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 3 Shikinaisha in the province – 0 Shikinai Shosha and 3 Shikinai Taisha.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451199]]

[[:ja:安芸国の式内社一覧]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:27, 16 February 2026 (UTC)}}
This is a list of Shikinaisha in [[Aki Province]]. That is the shrines in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] in the province.

The Engishiki Jinmyōchō counts 3 Shikinaisha in the province – 0 Shikinai Shosha and 3 Shikinai Taisha.
